entry.title: Re: End-to-End Setup of SAP Cloud ALM for Operations – La...
entry.content:
Dear @S0018757939,

SAP Cloud ALM setup for Public Cloud DEV / QAS / PROD should be split into two parts:

  • Operations and
  • Implementation / Deployment Governance

For Operations, each S/4HANA Cloud tenant should be connected separately to SAP Cloud ALM. In practice, this means DEV, QAS and PROD each need their own communication setup. The technical connection is based on the SAP Cloud ALM service key and communication arrangements in S/4HANA Cloud

The main arrangement for Operations monitoring is SAP_COM_0527 – Application Monitoring Push Integration. It is used to push monitoring data from S/4HANA Cloud to SAP Cloud ALM. For Business Process Monitoring, an additional arrangement SAP_COM_0523 – Business Process Monitoring Push Integration is required

More details below:

SAP Learning – Connecting SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition to SAP Cloud ALM for Operations

The monitoring setup should then be completed in SAP Cloud ALM for the required use cases:

  • Health Monitoring – technical health of DEV, QAS and PROD
  • Integration & Exception Monitoring – failed messages, exceptions and integration issues
  • Job & Automation Monitoring – job status, runtime and delays
  • Business Process Monitoring – business KPIs and process exceptions
  • Real User Monitoring – real user request performance and usage

In PROD, alerting and responsibilities should be configured carefully. For DEV and QAS I would keep the setup lighter and mainly use it for visibility and testing

More details below:

SAP Help – Health Monitoring
SAP Help – Integration & Exception Monitoring
SAP Help – Job & Automation Monitoring
SAP Help – Real User Monitoring
SAP Learning – Business Process Monitoring

For user onboarding, users should first be created or imported in SAP Cloud Identity Services / IAS. After that they should be added to SAP Cloud ALM and assigned the required SAP Cloud ALM roles. Project roles are handled separately inside the implementation project

More details below:

SAP Help – User Management in SAP Cloud ALM
SAP Help – SAP Cloud ALM Roles

For the Implementation part, create an SAP Cloud ALM project for the S/4HANA Cloud implementation. Then maintain the project scope, workstreams, roles, teams, tags and project members. A system group should be created for the landscape, for example S4HC_DEV_QAS_PROD and then assigned to a deployment plan

More details below:

SAP Help – SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation
SAP Help – Projects and Setup
SAP Help – System Groups and Deployment Plans

For Transport Management, SAP Cloud ALM should be used mainly as the governance and tracking layer for requirements, features, releases, deployments and transport visibility. The actual Public Cloud transport execution remains aligned with the Public Cloud transport process. For ATO-related transports, SAP Cloud ALM can be used to track deployment through the implementation landscape

More details below:

SAP Help – Enabling Transport Management
SAP Help – SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Adaptation Transport Organizer

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Existe forma de registrar no DRC(Public) notas históricas já autorizados em outro sistema?
entry.content:
Olá, pessoal!

Estou passando por uma situação no SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (release 2602) no Brasil, e chegamos em um cenário que não encontramos documentado em nenhum lugar. Se alguém do time de DRC puder ajudar, agradeço muito.

**Cenário**
O cliente operava em um sistema legado (não-SAP) antes desta implantação. Esse sistema legado emitiu e transmitiu notas fiscais de saída (NF-e modelo 55) que já foram autorizadas pela SEFAZ em períodos fiscais anteriores (ou seja, já possuem chave de acesso, protocolo de autorização e XML autorizado pela SEFAZ).

O cliente agora precisa que esses documentos históricos apareçam no monitor fiscal do novo sistema S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (app Administrar Documentos Eletrônicos / SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, opção de faturamento de saída para o Brasil) — unicamente para fins de registro interno e continuidade de auditoria. Não queremos retransmitir esses documentos à SEFAZ, já que eles foram autorizados sob uma numeração/série diferente, gerada pelo sistema legado.

**Perguntas**
1. Existe algum relatório, app ou objeto de migração padrão para registrar documentos de saída (NF-e e possivelmente CT-e/NFS-e) já autorizados, no monitor DRC do Brasil, sem disparar transmissão à SEFAZ?
2. A funcionalidade "Upload Electronic Documents to eDocument Cockpit" (documentação DC_NFe) se aplica a esse cenário, ou tem outra finalidade?

Qualquer indicação — documentação oficial, SAP Notes ou experiência real de projeto — ajudaria muito. Desde já, agradeço!

entry.title: Re: Merge not working
entry.content:
Hi!

After triggering a Merge from a parallel line into the main line, the workspace may remain locked with the following message: "Your workspace is locked due to an activation process."

When checking the Deployment Target tab, the deployment may remain in In Progress. Although this may look like a stuck Merge, in most cases it is actually the expected system behavior.

The first thing to verify is the Customer staging in the Display Business Configuration Stagings app. In most cases, the Merge is simply waiting for the required manual activation to be completed.

During a Merge process, SAP Central Business Configuration deploys two different staging types.

1. SAP Central Business Configuration staging: This staging transfers the SAP Best Practices delta between the workspaces.

2. Customer staging

  • This staging transfers the customer-specific configuration changes.
  • Unlike the CBC staging, Customer staging requires manual activation before the Merge can be finalized.

As described in the SAP Help Portal Merging (Process) | SAP Help Portal during Merge, the configuration is deployed to SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. As a next step, you need to open the Display Business Configuration Stagings app and activate the configuration in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Until the Customer staging reaches the status Staging Completed, the workspace remains locked.

Before activating the staging, ensure that your user has the business catalog: SAP_CORE_BC_CBC_STA_PC assigned to the appropriate business role.

To finalize and process correctly the Merge, you can follow the steps in the SAP Help Portal Content Deployment and Activation (Merge) or KBA3775313 - Workspace locked with message "Your workspace is locked due to an activation process" after Merge in SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me

Once the Customer staging reaches Staging Completed, the Merge finishes and the workspace lock is released automatically.

Best Regards!

entry.title: Application Deletion in SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG) – Practical Approach
entry.content:
Introduction

During one of the SAP Identity Access Governance (IAG) implementation projects, I encountered a common challenge related to application cleanup in test systems before UAT.

Typically, we integrate multiple SAP cloud and on-premises applications into the IAG landscape during the initial phases in the test environment, followed by reconfiguration in production after successful completion of UT, SIT, and UAT cycles.

In real project scenarios, however, the test system often ends up with additional applications created for temporary purposes such as proof of concept (POC), testing, or initial integration validation.

 

Business Challenge

Before starting UAT, clients expect a clean IAG environment without unnecessary:

  • Applications
  • Access/request data
  • Business roles
  • Users and role assignments

However, earlier SAP guidance (for example, SAP Note 2817560) indicated that applications could not be deleted once used, primarily due to audit considerations.

This created a challenge in maintaining a clean and controlled UAT environment.

 

Solution Overview

SAP has now addressed this limitation by introducing a role collection:

CIAG_Connector_Deletion_Administration

This role collection enables administrators to delete applications along with all associated data in SAP IAG.

 

Step-by-Step Approach

Step 1: Clean Up Risk Analysis Data

Before deleting an application, it is important to remove risk analysis dependencies.

  • Navigate to Administration → Applications tile
  • Select the relevant application
  • Remove the Business Function Group association

  • Run the "Access Analysis" job via Job Scheduler

This ensures removal of

  • Role-level risk analysis results
  • User-level risk analysis results

Note: This step does not delete the ruleset or business function group itself. If required, those can be deleted separately from the Business Function Groups tile.

 

Step 2: Delete the Application and Associated Data

  • Navigate to Administration → Applications tile
  • Select the application to be deleted
  • Click on Delete button at bottom right corner

  • Enter the application name for confirmation
  • Click Delete button

System Behavior

  • A message appears indicating deletion is in progress
  • After completion, the application is removed from the list
  • On refresh, the application is no longer visible

You can validate the cleanup through

  • Maintain User Data → to check user-related data
  • Access Maintenance → to verify role/access cleanup

 

Important Considerations

  • Avoid mapping this role collection to SAP IAS groups that are requestable via IAG, in case SAP IAS is also one of the target applications
  • Do not assign this role in production environments, as production data should not be deleted to comply with audit requirements.
  • In test systems:
    • Assign only temporarily
    • Remove immediately after use to prevent accidental deletion

 

Key learnings

  • Application deletion in SAP IAG is now possible with controlled authorization
  • Proper cleanup (risk data, associations) is critical before deletion
  • This feature is especially useful for:
    • UAT preparation
    • Cleaning up POC integrations
    • Maintaining governance in test systems

entry.title: Integrating SAP Concur with SAP IAG – Implementation Guide and Key Learnings
entry.content:
Introduction

SAP IAG Enterprise (Standard) edition provides out-of-the-box integration with SAP Concur for user provisioning and access governance. However, during implementation, it has been observed that certain areas require deeper understanding beyond standard documentation.

This blog covers:

  • Detailed configuration steps
  • Important technical considerations
  • Practical challenges faced during implementation

The intent is to provide a simple and practical guide for SAP IAG consultants working on SAP Concur integration.

 

Initial SAP IAG System Setup

Before starting the integration, ensure the following setup is completed:

  1. SAP BTP setup: Global Account → Subaccount → SAP IAG Enterprise Edition subscription (Test/Prod)
  2. Trust configuration is completed with SAP Cloud Identity Services (CIS), including attribute mapping
  3. Standard SAP IAG groups are created in SAP CIS, such as: IAG_USER, IAG_WF_MANAGER, IAG_WF_ADMIN, IAG_RAA_DEFAULT, etc.
  4. If the manager is used as an approval stage in access requests, the user’s manager must be assigned with the IAG_WF_MANAGER group so that it is automatically derived in the access request form. Otherwise, the requester needs to manually select the manager from users assigned to this group
  5. Role collection to CIS group mapping is configured (for example): CIAG_Access_Request, CIAG_Access_Analysis mapped to a custom CIS group like ZIAG_Access_Requestor.
  6. Access to SAP IAG with administrator privileges
  7. User data source is configured using SAP Identity Services Identity Directory or Identity Authentication Service via IPS_PROXY V2.
  8. Application parameters and master data are properly maintained
  9. User and group synchronization is configured using SCIM
  10. Mail server is configured for outbound emails (Destinations: parameters_destination and bpmworkflowruntime_mail)

 

SAP Concur to SAP IAG Integration and Configuration Steps

The following steps outline the end-to-end configuration required to integrate SAP Concur with SAP IAG after completing the initial system setup.

 

Step 1: User Provisioning Service V4 (UPSv4)

User Provisioning Service V4 (UPSv4) is an SAP Concur API framework that enables SAP IAG to automate user provisioning and lifecycle management in SAP Concur.

To use the User Provisioning Service (UPSv4) API, the client must have Concur Expense, Concur Travel, or both.

Clients using Concur Expense Professional/Premium Edition and/or Concur Request Professional/Premium Edition need to get UPS enabled through their Concur Client Executive (Sales Representative).

In addition, the client must subscribe to Client Web Services after signing the web services agreement. This will then be included as part of the SAP Concur contract.

For API details, refer to:

  • SAP API Hub – Concur User Provisioning
  • Concur Developer Documentation – User Provisioning v4

There are also certain limitations with UPS, which can be referred to in SAP Note:

  • SAP Note 3363157

 

Step 2: Configure OAuth2 authentication application and Generate company refresh token and UUID

Once the User Provisioning Service (UPSv4) is enabled, the next step is to configure OAuth2 authentication for SAP Concur integration.

This step establishes secure API-based authentication between SAP IAG and SAP Concur using OAuth2 credentials and tokens.

This involves two key activities:

Step 2.1: Configure OAuth2 Application

This helps to create authentication credentials (Client ID/Secret) and defines required API access scopes.

  • Create an OAuth2 authentication application in SAP Concur
  • Generate Client ID and Client Secret
  • Maintain the required grants and scopes

Step 2.2: Generate Company Token and UUID

This provides tenant-specific identifiers and tokens required for API communication with SAP Concur.

  • Generate the Company Request Token
  • Obtain: Token Service URL, Refresh Token, Company UUID

A detailed reference for this setup is available in the SAP Community blog by @tim_chapman24 

  • Introduction to Web Services for Concur Expense Authentication

Additional reference:

  • Concur Authentication API Documentation

Important Configuration Points

  • While configuring the OAuth2 application, ensure that the required grants and scopes are maintained correctly
  • Missing grant or scope may result in 403 (Forbidden) errors in provisioning logs

For SAP IAG integration, the following grants can be maintained:

  • refresh_token
  • password

For more details, refer to:

  • Refresh Token Grant
  • Password Grant

Scopes to be Maintained

Below are the commonly required scopes:

 

Scope

Scope Description

identity.user.core.read

Read user core data

identity.user.coresensitive.read

Read core sensitive data

identity.user.emails.verified.writeonly

Write access for verified email

identity.user.enterprise.read

Read user enterprise data

identity.user.coreenterprise.writeonly

Write access to all core and enterprise fields except external ID

identity.user.externalID.writeonly

Write access to external ID only

identity.user.ids.read

Read user ID data

identity.user.sap.read

Read a user’s SAP Global ID. Used for intra SAP service communication

identity.user.sap.writeonly

Write a user’s SAP Global ID. Used for intra SAP service communication

spend.user.general.read

View spend user information

spend.user.general.writeonly

Change spend user information

travel.user.general.read

Read general Travel data: Travel approval manager, Travel Name, Rule Class, Groups, Org Unit, Travel Custom Fields

travel.user.private.read

Read private Travel data: CRS Name/GDS Sync ID, Travel Name Remark, Gender

user.provision.read

Request status of a provisioning request

user.provision.write

Provision a user

 

Required Permissions in SAP Concur

Ensure that the Concur administrator has the following permissions:

Activity

Permission

Configure OAuth2 authentication Application

Web Services Administrator

Generate Company Request Token and Company UUID

Web Services Administrator

User Creation and Maintenance

Employee Administrator

Permission Assignment and Administration

Role Administrator

 

Step 3: Destination Setup in BTP Subaccount

This step defines the connectivity configuration in SAP BTP to enable SAP IAG to communicate with SAP Concur APIs.

Create a destination for SAP Concur in the BTP subaccount where the SAP IAG service is subscribed.

Maintain the destination with the following details:

Property Key

Values

Name

CONCUR (or any other preferred name)

Type

HTTP

Description

Concur Destination (or any other preferred description)

URL

<Enter SAP Concur API URL> For example: https://us.api.concursolutions.com  (Refer to https://me.sap.com/notes/2914977/E for Concur URLs)

ProxyType

Internet

Authentication

OAuth2RefreshToken

Use mTLS for token retrieval

<Unchecked>

Client ID

Enter the generated Concur Client ID in Step 2.1

Client Secret

Enter the generated Concur Client Secret in Step 2.1

Token Service URL Type

Dedicated

Token Service URL

Enter the SAP Concur token URL in Step 2.2

CompanyEntityCode

Registered Company Entity Code can be obtained from SAP Concur team

CompanyUUID

Generated Company UUID in Step 2.2 (will be used as prefix to user ID in SAP Concur)

RefreshToken

Generated refresh token in Step 2.2

Use default client truststore

<Checked>

 

Important Note: The Company Entity Code is used as a prefix for the user ID during provisioning in SAP Concur from SAP IAG. As a result, the user ID is created in the format: <user_ID>@<CompanyEntityCode>

 

Step 4: Create an Application in SAP IAG

This step registers SAP Concur as a managed application in SAP IAG to enable access governance and provisioning.

Log in to SAP IAG and navigate to: Administration → Application

Click on the ‘+’ icon to create a new application and maintain the following details:

Field

Value

Application Name

CONCUR (or Any other preferred name)

Description

SAP Concur Application (or any other preferred description)

Application Type

SAP Concur

HCP Destination

<BTP destination created above. Provide exactly same name of BTP destination.>

 

Mandatory Custom Fields

Once the application is created, SAP IAG automatically creates four mandatory custom fields.
These can be verified under: Administration → Custom Fields

Name

Description

Label

Input Type

Data Type

Field Length

Required

Allowed values

COUNTRY

Spend User Country

Country

Input Text

String

2

Yes

A two-letter country code defined in ISO 3166-1 alpha-2

F(e.g. GB for UK, AU for Australia, US for United States of America, etc.)

LOCALE

Spend User Locale

Spend-User Locale

Input Text

String

5

Yes

Required when using Spend User extension. Valid locale from the list of configured locales as defined in [RFC5646]. But here for Concur, the locale will be an ISO 639 language code with an ISO 3166 country/region code, separated by a underscore (e.g., en_US, en_AU, de_DE, etc.)

REIMBURSEMENT_CURRENCY

SpendUser Reimbursement Currency

Reimbursement Currency

Input Text

String

3

Yes

Valid three digit currency code in the list of system reimbursement currencies. Ex. AUD, USD, etc.

REIMBURSEMENT_TYPE

SpendUser Reimbursement Type

Reimbursement Type

Input Text

String

20

Yes

The reimbursement type for the user. Supported values: ACCOUNTS_PAYABLE, ADP_PAYROLL, CONCUR_PAY, OTHER.

 

Important Points

  • These custom fields will appear in the access request form for SAP Concur
  • All four fields are mandatory for both new and existing users
  • Values must be provided in the correct format, otherwise provisioning will fail

Even though SAP IAG provides an option to edit or deactivate these fields under Custom Fields, it is recommended not to change them, as they are required for Concur provisioning.

 

Additional Note

SAP is introducing additional custom fields to support more Concur attributes. You can track updates here: SAP Roadmap Explorer – SAP IAG Concur Enhancements

 

Step 5: Run Repository Sync Job

This step helps to synchronize users and access (roles/profiles) from SAP Concur into SAP IAG for governance processes.

After creating the application, run the Repository Sync job to fetch data from SAP Concur.

Navigate to Administration → Job Scheduler and execute the Repository Sync job for the SAP Concur application

Once the job is completed successfully, user and access data from SAP Concur are synchronized to SAP IAG.

You can verify the synchronized data in:

  • Maintain User Data
  • Access Maintenance (use filters for SAP Concur)

User Data

  • SAP Concur user ID is displayed in the format: <user_id>@<CompanyEntityCode>
  • The <user_id> corresponds to the login name maintained in SAP CIS

Access Data

Under Access Maintenance, two types of access are available for SAP Concur:

  1. Concur Product: Represents Concur profiles such as Expense, Invoice, Reporting, Request, and Travel
  2. Single Roles: Represents authorization roles such as EXP_APPROVER, EXP_ATTENDEE_ADMIN, EXP_CONFIG_ADMIN_CLIENT, etc.

Both access types can be requested via SAP IAG. Additional attributes such as: Business Process, Subprocess, Criticality, Alias, Assignment Approvers etc. can be maintained to control workflow routing and approval determination.

Note:
Only users assigned with the IAG_RAA_DEFAULT group are available for role assignment approval.

 

Step 6: Configure Access Risk Ruleset

This step helps to enable risk analysis by importing and activating SAP Concur-specific segregation of duties rules.

For risk analysis, the standard GLOBAL ruleset for SAP Concur needs to be requested.

  • Create an SAP Support case for component: GRC-IAG-AA (Access Analysis Service)
  • Provide required access (IAS Administration) to SAP to upload the ruleset

Once the ruleset is uploaded:

  • A business function group SAP_CONCUR is created
  • All SAP Concur-related risks are available under this group

Ruleset Behavior

  • SAP Concur roles are standard and cannot be customized
  • Risks are defined based on conflicting role combinations

Example:

Post ruleset setup, validate the risk count against the business function group and run the Access Analysis job from the Job Scheduler.

After completion of Access Analysis job User-level risks can be checked in ‘Access Analysis’ application and role-level risks can be checked in ‘Access Maintenance’ application.

             

Step 7: User ID mapping

This step aligns user identities across systems to avoid duplicates and ensure accurate license consumption.

User ID mapping is important for license optimization in SAP IAG. The Monitored User List in Maintain User Data shows users consuming IAG licenses

Key Consideration

SAP Concur uses a different user ID format: <user_ID>@<prefix>

If multiple applications are integrated with SAP IAG:

  • The same user may appear as different users due to different ID formats
  • This can lead to duplicate license consumption

SAP IAG supports auto user ID mapping for most applications Refer: SAP Note 3553352 (Auto-Generated Entries in User ID Mapping) however, SAP Concur is not supported for auto-mapping

Hence to avoid duplicate license consumption user ID mapping must be maintained manually. This requires additional monitoring as part of ongoing operations for new user creation and also increases administrative effort.

 

How to Maintain Mapping

  • Navigate to: Administration → Maintain User ID Mapping
  • Mapping can be maintained: Individually or via Bulk upload through CSV file
  • Master User ID → Login name from user source and Mapped ID → SAP Concur User ID

 

Additional Notes

  • Other IAG services (Access Request, Workflow, Role Design, Access Analysis, Access Certification) work similarly to other applications
  • Privileged Access Management (PAM) Service is not supported for SAP Concur

 

Key Learnings and Limitations

Based on implementation experience, below are some important points to be aware of:

1. Provisioning Behavior (Asynchronous Processing)

Provisioning in SAP Concur works in an asynchronous manner.

  • Step 1: When the access request is submitted, the first run of the provisioning job shows the status as “In Process”
  • Step 2: In the next job run, the final status is updated as “Successful” or “Failed” based on the actual result

So, the provisioning result is not available immediately in the first run.

2. Manual User ID Mapping Required

As SAP Concur does not support automated user ID mapping:

  • Manual mapping is required in SAP IAG
  • Continuous monitoring is needed for new users

Without this, duplicate users may be created, leading to additional license consumption.

3. Mandatory Custom Fields

All four custom fields are mandatory:

  • COUNTRY
  • LOCALE
  • REIMBURSEMENT_CURRENCY
  • REIMBURSEMENT_TYPE

If any field is left empty or deactivated, provisioning will fail.

4. No Validation for Custom Fields

Custom fields accept only specific values (mentioned in step 4) and formats (case-sensitive).

However:

  • Validation is not enforced at the request submission stage, which may require additional checks during provisioning
  • Users can submit incorrect values
  • Request goes through full approval workflow
  • Failure happens only during provisioning job execution.

In addition:

  • Logs may require additional analysis to identify the exact issue
  • Administrators need to analyze logs to find the problem

 5. No Pre-population of Custom Fields

  • Custom fields need to be maintained for both new and existing users
  • There is no pre-filled data even for existing users

This requires proper end-user training before rollout.

 6. Partial User Creation in case of errors

If incorrect values are provided in custom fields user may still get created in SAP Concur but no profiles or roles are assigned

Such users are difficult to find using standard user search and may require support from SAP Concur functional team to verify at database level.

Users who exist in SAP Concur but do not have any profiles (Expense, Travel, etc.) will still appear in Maintain User Data and will be counted for SAP IAG licensing.

7. No Bulk Provisioning Support

SAP IAG currently does not support bulk user provisioning/deprovisioning for SAP Concur.

Recommended approach:

  • Use SAP ICS (Integration with SAP Concur Solutions) / SAP IPS for user creation with basic roles and/or user account termination/deactivation.
  • Use SAP IAG only for role-based access requests

8. Option for HR-Driven Provisioning

Integration with SuccessFactors can be used to trigger user creation/termination and manage lifecycle and provisioning automatically.

9. Refresh Token Handling

  • Refresh token used in BTP destination has a validity of 6 months
  • SAP IAG / SAP IPS automatically refreshes the token during provisioning job execution

No manual intervention is required in normal scenarios.

10. Standard Risk Rules May Need Adjustment

The Global ruleset for SAP Concur includes some risks which may not be relevant because SAP Concur has built-in controls for certain scenarios to prevent risks.

Such risks can be reviewed with business and deactivated, if required.

 

Overall, the integration is manageable, but attention to detail is important, especially around custom fields and user mapping. This guidance is based on practical implementation experience, highlighting what worked well and the challenges encountered during execution.

Going forward, SAP may address some of these challenges, such as improvements in custom field handling, automated user ID mapping, more relevant global rulesets, and clearer provisioning logs, which should simplify the integration further.

If you have faced similar challenges or have additional insights, feel free to share your experience in the comments.

entry.title: Re: No Project report with correct Budget available (Budg...
entry.content:
Hello!

1.An internal/overhead work order settling to a WBS element posts the order's actual costs to the WBS via a settlement cost element.

2. The settled transaction is correctly reflected here. This confirms the underlying CO/FI posting from settlement is correct and reached the WBS, the issue is isolated to the analytical reporting layer, not the settlement transaction itself.

3. Per SAP KBA 2799100 and KBA 3453098, these apps calculate actual cost from a separate reporting layer (not directly from the AVC engine). They only display costs posted to GL accounts correctly mapped, in Financial Statement Version YPS2, to an FS item carrying the semantic tag ACT_COST (or a child tag whose parent is ACT_COST), positioned under: Contribution Margin Report - P&L before Tax - CM III - CM II - CM I - Cost of Goods Sold.

4. Monitor Projects reads directly from the live AVC engine, the same engine that triggered the 100% budget error. This is why it correctly reflects the work order's settled cost consumption. However, it only surfaces this at the project header level, not drilled down to individual WBS elements.

5. Monitor Projects (Item 4) is the more reliable figure for actual budget availability, because it is fed directly by the live AVC engine that also produced the 100% error. Project Budget Report / Project Cost Report (Item 3) actual-cost calculation depends on the FSV/semantic-tag mapping described above. Monitor Projects only provides the project-level aggregate, not the WBS-level detail required for granular budget control.

7. Once the FSV/semantic tag mapping is corrected:

- Project Budget Report (Overview + Line Items) - Primary AVC report at both WBS and project (rolled-up) level: total budget, commitment, actual cost, AVC/available budget remaining. Includes assigned work order commitment/actual costs in the project hierarchy even before settlement.

- Project Cost Report- Total plan/actual/commitment cost, including assigned work order commitment costs, drillable to line-item detail, shown in the project hierarchy.

- Monitor Projects - Project-level AVC monitoring/dashboard is useful as a cross-check on the aggregate figure, not for WBS-level breakdown.

- Project Financial Controller Overview – serves as an entry dashboard navigating into the above apps.

8. At settlement: purchase requisition, purchase order, and activity confirmation postings create commitment and actual cost on the work order itself, not on the WBS. The WBS's AVC control object is only consumed once the work order's actual cost is settled to the WBS. The AVC 100% error was triggered at the point of settlement, not earlier in the PR/PO/confirmation cycle.

Recommended approach:

  • Identify the GL account/settlement cost element posted when WO 21000042 settles to the WBS (from the settlement document).
  • In FSV YPS2  confirm this GL account's FS item sits under Contribution Margin Report -P&L before Tax - CM III - CM II - CM I - Cost of Goods Sold, and is not a balance sheet account (unless it's project stock, which follows a separate rule).
  • Confirm the FS item carries tag ACT_COST or a child tag with ACT_COST as parent, via "Define Semantic Tags for Financial Statement Versions" / "Assign Semantic Tags to Financial Statement Versions."
  • Confirm the settlement posting's AccountAssignmentType is 'PR'.
  • Confirm the ledger posted to is Leading Ledger.
  • Run app F1478 "Replicate Runtime Hierarchy" for YPS2 after any FSV/semantic-tag change.
  • Re-run Project Budget Report and Project Cost Report at WBS level to confirm actual cost and AVC remaining now populate and reconcile with Monitor Projects.
  • If discrepancy between Project Budget Report and Monitor Projects persists after all checks, escalate to SAP Support mentioning KBA 2799100 and KBA 3453098.

entry.title: EWM: Split 1 wave item for direct picking and 2 step picking at the same time
entry.content:
Hi folks, 

Here with the customer we have the following scenario regarding the way that the operation needs to pick the product in his warehouse. We are on EWM S/4 HANA 2023 btw.

Basically, the scenario consists of:

1 PAL = 20 CAR

- One wave item is created from a warehouse request that has 1 PAL and 5 CAR on it for store 1. 

- The second wave item has 15 CAR on it for a store 2.

So using the 2 step picking optimization functionality we are looking to split the first wave item to create one warehouse task for 1 PAL in order to be picked directly to the stage and the rest (5 CAR) of that wave item allow to add it to the second wave item to be picked as 2 step picking.

However, after looking into BADIs or the standard options, we are not seeing a way to do that without splitting the Outbound delivery item during its creation on EWM, but again, there is no a way to do that via standard or badi method.

So do you know how can we resolve that? what are you thoughts on this? 

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Build Code-based AI Agents on SAP Business AI Platform (Tokyo, Japan }
entry.content:
In this CodeJam, you will learn how to build state-of-the-art AI agents using the Generative AI Hub and Python. You will also gain the skills to create custom tools for your agents, including leveraging SAP's RPT-1 model. Finally, you will deploy your agents to SAP BTP.

In this CodeJam you will build your agents with Python and CrewAI.

The CodeJam will include the following SAP technologies:

- Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core

- SAP RPT-1

- Grounding Service

- SAP AI Launchpad

- Business Application Studio (Python)

Exercise Content:  https://github.com/SAP-samples/codejam-code-based-agents

Prerequisites: 

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter and have a supported Chromium-based browser
  • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, if you have one, to have the instructions on a separate screen.

Location and Date:

This is a free in-person event and is planned for Monday, September 28, in Tokyo from 10:00 AM to 5:00 PM local time. The event's language and content will be in English. 

Event Address:

Sigmaxyz Training center | Kamiya-cho, Minato-ku, Tokyo
  •  Special thanks to SAP Japan Community for hosting this event.
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @Shilpa_Shankar & @sheenamk 

Please note that spaces are limited! We can only accommodate 40 attendees. If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right. About 2 weeks before the event, you will receive an email to confirm your attendance and will receive further instructions.

entry.title: SAC Excel Add-in - Unable to Maintain Cost Center & GL Account Row Filters from Planning Form Export
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m running into an issue with a SAC planning form that we are exporting to the SAC Excel Add-in, and I’m hoping someone has seen this before or found a workaround.

We have a planning form in SAC where users plan by Cost Center and G/L Account. In the SAC form itself, users are able to work with the hierarchy and select specific nodes or choose the members they want under those nodes.

However, once the same form is opened in the SAC Excel Add-in, the behavior changes.

The main issue is that users are not able to properly maintain the filters for Cost Center and G/L Account in Rows.

What makes this more complicated is:

  1. Cost Centers are assigned to users through security/data access control
  2. Some G/L Accounts are locked intentionally because they are planned in the Workforce model and crossed over into the Income Statement model
  3. In the SAC form, users can select specific nodes and effectively narrow down what they want to work on
  4. In the Excel Add-in, that same flexibility is not available. Instead of being able to cherry-pick members under a node, users end up getting all Cost Centers or all G/L Accounts under that selection

We are also seeing messages in the Excel Add-in related to complex filters, and in some cases the filter cannot be displayed or maintained in the filter dialog.

What I’m trying to understand is:

  • Is this a known limitation of the SAC Excel Add-in when the source table/form contains hierarchy-based or complex filters?
  • Does security on Cost Center impact how the Excel Add-in handles row filtering?
  • Is there any workaround that would still allow users to select specific Cost Centers or G/L Accounts instead of bringing in everything under the node?
  • Has anyone found a better design approach for planning forms that need to be used both in SAC and in the Excel Add-in?

Our goal is to let users work in Excel, but still keep the same controlled planning experience they have in SAC as much as possible.

Any ideas, recommendations, or workaround suggestions would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Free Goods Determination API which can be use for Dat...
entry.content:
Hi Abulhasnat,

 

Standard SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud does not provide a dedicated, direct API for bulk maintaining Free Goods Determination (VBN2). You cannot wrap a custom BAPI around it like in On-Premise.

 

You have two standard alternatives:

1. Price Management API: Map your free goods logic as a 100% discount. Use the Condition Record for Pricing in Sales API (API_SLSPRICINGCONDITIONRECORD_SRV). Your third-party system can push the condition records directly here.

2. Sales Order A2X Payload: If the external system generates the order, handle the logic externally. Pass the free item (e.g., Item Category TANN) with the 100% discount already applied when calling the Sales Order (A2X) API.

entry.title: Re: Update settlement rule of maintenance order
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

The issue with EXIT_SAPLCOI1_027 is exactly as your developer noted - it is tightly coupled to the SAP GUI and won't consistently trigger across WebDynpro or Fiori (UI5) interfaces.

 

For Maintenance Orders in modern SAP, you have two main options:

Option 1: Standard EAM Configuration - Before coding, verify if you can map the receiver directly in standard Settlement Profile configuration based on the Equipment/Functional Location category.

Option 2: The WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI - If custom logic is mandatory, use the WORKORDER_UPDATE BAdI, specifically the AT_RELEASE or BEFORE_UPDATE methods. This operates at the database update level, ensuring it triggers regardless of whether the order was released via Fiori, GUI, or an API call.

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA Cloud: Trigger outbound HTTP call to Feishu /...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, you cannot directly embed an outbound HTTP REST call (like a webhook to Feishu) inside a standard Flexible Workflow step.

 

The standard architectural approach requires decoupling the event from the action:

1. Enterprise Event Enablement: Configure your S/4HANA system to emit a standard business event when the specific workflow is approved.

2. SAP BTP Integration: Subscribe to this event using SAP Event Mesh or SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration).

3. The Feishu Call: Build the integration flow in Cloud Integration to catch the event, transform the SAP JSON payload into the Feishu-compatible format, and trigger the outbound HTTP POST to the Feishu Integration Platform.

entry.title: Re: Request for Bulk Partial Payment Posting Solution SAP...
entry.content:
Hi Ankit140,

 

Let's save you some time: there is no standard, out-of-the-box Fiori app in S/4HANA Public Cloud designed specifically for bulk uploading and posting partial payments via Excel. Fiori apps like Post Incoming Payments (F1345) and Clear Incoming Payments are built for manual, individual processing. Unless you can adapt your process to rely purely on standard Electronic Bank Statement (EBS) interpretation algorithms for mass automated clearing, standard config won't cover this.

 

To achieve an Excel-driven mass partial clearing process, you are looking at custom development. Typically, you would need to build a custom Fiori application that parses your specific Excel template and orchestrates the clearing logic via the Journal Entry API (API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV).

entry.title: Re: How do you handle sensitive data when integrating ext...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

Integrating external GenAI with SAP requires strict architectural boundaries to prevent PII leakage. Standard direct API calls from S/4HANA to public LLMs (like OpenAI) are highly discouraged for enterprise data.

 

Here is the standard architectural approach to secure the data:

1. SAP Generative AI Hub (BTP): This is the primary standard path. It provides tenant isolation, enterprise data privacy agreements (where prompts are not used to train the base models), and central credential management.

2. Data Redaction Middleware: Before any S/4HANA payload hits the LLM endpoint, pass it through SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) or an external middleware layer designed to scrub PII (using regex or NER models) so the AI only receives anonymized context.

entry.title: Re: Equipment User Status Functionality in SAP S/4HANA Pu...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, defining traditional User Status Profiles (the equivalent of OIBS in On-Premise) and assigning them to Equipment categories is fully supported.

 

You can configure this via the Manage Your Solution or Central Business Configuration (CBC) app:

1. Search for the configuration activity Define User Status Profile (SSCUI 103250). Here you define your specific statuses and the business transactions they are allowed to restrict.

2. Then, use the activity Assign User Status Profile to Equipment Category (SSCUI 103248) to link the profile to your specific equipment types.

entry.title: Re: BAPI_PO_CHANGE – Custom PO Item fields not updated vi...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

The most common culprit when BAPI_PO_CHANGE ignores custom fields in EXTENSIONIN (despite correct append structures like BAPI_TE_MEPOITEM) is a missing or incomplete mapping in the standard BAdI.

 

You must implement the BAdI ME_BAPI_PO_CUST. Specifically, use the method MAP2I_EXTENSIONIN to map the custom fields from the BAPI_TE_MEPOITEM structure to the internal PO structures (like EKPO). Without this BAdI active and coded, the BAPI fundamentally does not know where to push the data from the extension container to the database, and it will silently ignore the update without throwing an error in the return table.

entry.title: Re: SAP API Restriction: Only Purchase Orders with Docume...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

Let's save you some time: yes, this is a known and frequently encountered restriction in standard S/4HANA Public Cloud. The standard API_PURCHASEORDER_PROCESS_SRV OData service rigidly validates against standard document types (like NB) when processing extensibility logic via standard BO nodes.

 

If it is outright blocking or ignoring your Z* document types, you generally have to pivot away from using the standard OData service for those specific custom objects. The standard alternative is utilizing Developer Extensibility (ABAP Environment) to create a custom RAP wrapper that handles the custom document type and explicitly orchestrates the updates using EML (MODIFY ENTITIES).

entry.title: Re: Excel Upload Functionality for Payment Entries in SAP
entry.content:
Hi Indira,

 

Standard S/4HANA Public Cloud does not natively support free-text Excel uploads for incoming payments without strict formatting. The standard Fiori apps are generally designed for manual clearing or interpreting standard bank files.

 

You have two standard options to handle mass uploads:

1. Convert to Standard Bank Format: Instead of a raw Excel upload, convert your Excel data into an MT940 or CAMT.053 format via an external script. You can then use the standard Manage Bank Messages or Manage Incoming Payment Files apps to process the clearing automatically using standard EBS interpretation algorithms.

2. Custom Fiori App (API-based): Build a custom Fiori application that parses the Excel file and pushes the entries into SAP via the Journal Entry - Post API (API_JOURNALENTRYITEMBASIC_SRV).

 

The tradeoff is complexity. Converting to MT940 relies on standard SAP clearing rules but requires external file manipulation. The custom Fiori app gives you a seamless UI but introduces development overhead.

entry.title: Re: Maintain PIR app and splitting indicator in S4 Hana p...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

In S/4HANA Public Cloud, you will notice that the legacy R/3 plant-level splitting indicator configuration is not available in the same way. The standard Maintain PIRs app natively expects you to maintain the periods directly (e.g., Weekly) rather than relying on SAP to automatically split a monthly upload into weekly buckets post-upload.

 

To handle weekly planning without manual math, here is the standard workaround process:

1. Update Material Master: Ensure the material's forecast period indicator is set to Weekly (W) in the MRP 3 view.

2. Direct Weekly Upload: The Maintain PIRs app supports a 52-week maintenance horizon. You must format your Excel upload file to contain weekly quantities *before* you upload it to the app.

3. Use SAP IBP (Alternative): If your business strictly forecasts monthly, the standard architectural recommendation is to use SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) to generate the forecast, split it into weekly buckets, and then integrate it back to S/4HANA Public Cloud as weekly PIRs.

entry.title: Re: How do I retrieve a CSRF token form SAP Integration S...
entry.content:
Hi there,

 

To securely retrieve and use an X-CSRF-Token in SAP Integration Suite (Cloud Integration) when calling an external OData service or an SAP backend API, you must decouple the fetch and the post into two steps.

 

Here is the exact step-by-step approach to configure this in your integration flow:

1. Create the Fetch Request: Add a Request Reply step with an HTTP/OData receiver adapter pointing to your target endpoint (or a lightweight entity). Set the method to GET.

2. Set the Request Header: In a Content Modifier *before* the GET call, add a Message Header named X-CSRF-Token and set its value to the constant string Fetch.

3. Capture the Response: When the GET call returns, the target system will include the generated token in the response headers.

4. Pass to the POST Call: In a subsequent Content Modifier, read the X-CSRF-Token from the incoming response headers and pass it as a Request Header into your actual POST/PUT HTTP receiver channel. Ensure you also pass back the Set-Cookie session cookies if the backend requires stateful validation.

entry.title: Re: Best practices for automating rule-based Material Mas...
entry.content:
Hi Berke,

 

Automating MM01 (or the Manage Mass Maintenance app) dynamically from Excel rules is a highly debated topic. For daily, rule-based operations, standard data migration tools (like the Migration Cockpit) are too cumbersome.

 

You basically have two modern standard options:

1. SAP Build Process Automation (iRPA): You can build a standard bot on BTP that reads the Excel file from a shared folder or email, parses the rules, and calls the standard Product Master (A2X) OData API (API_PRODUCT_SRV) to create the material.

2. Custom ABAP/RAP App: Build a custom Fiori elements application with an upload UI. The ABAP backend parses the Excel file and uses EML (MODIFY ENTITIES) to orchestrate the material creation and validation.

 

The tradeoff is licensing versus development effort. SAP Build is a low-code standard but requires BTP credits and can be brittle if your Excel format constantly changes. The custom RAP app handles complex validation perfectly but requires ABAP Cloud development overhead.

entry.title: Re: Rate Limiting on SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 APIs - Must to know
entry.content:
Hi Jilian

If the limit (150,000 + 10% (if increased)) reached, no new API calls reached to SAP V2. Those are rejected. We had this issue and we started limiting the API usage.

API Usage metrics:

User Menu -> System Settings -> Usage and Statistics -> API Metrics

If you don't find then you need to reach out SAP to enable. It is enabled by client request.

entry.title: Re: Advanced intercompany STO - Sales Area Mismatch in Er...
entry.content:
Hello!

Please, follow the instructions from OSS Note 2625459 - Error Message VP210

 

Cause:

The field 'Item Division' is flagged in Customizing of sales order type in the transaction VOV8 so the system proposes the division from the material master of the item and the division of material is not the same as the division of the ship-to-party.

If this flag is switched off, then the header division is accepted as the item division automatically. If this flag is switched on, then the item division is taken from material master.

Resolution:

- either switch off the flag 'Item Division' in Customizing of sales order type in transaction VOV8 (the division of sales order will be determined from the customer settings)

- or change the division of the material in the material master in transaction MM02 on the view 'Sales Screen 1'

- or define the customer for the same sales area as the material

- or make the relevant configuration in transaction VOR2 to use the common division (customizing table TVTA)

entry.title: Re: Importing users failed. Error is: Missing required di...
entry.content:
Root Cause is Display Name setting is checked as Required. 
Uncheck the tick box 

via Applications and resources>Tennant Settings > Authentication - login Alias > Required (remove check box for Required) 

Steps to replicate issue

  1. Download for IDP from S/4 Public Cloud Maintain Business User

  2. Open Admin Console

  3. Select Import Users 

  4. Select Bundled Applications - Productive System 

  5. Browse CSV File

  6. Click Import

  7. Error Occurs 

@Publiccloud

entry.title: Meet The Expert: Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts - Live Demo
entry.content:
We are excited to share details regarding an upcoming Live Demo on Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts. 

Aimed at existing SAP Ariba customers, this demo will provide a hands-on look at what’s new, what’s changing, and how to prepare for the transition. Through live product demos and real-world use cases, you’ll see exactly how Next-gen SAP Ariba differs from the classic experience, and the business value it can unlock.

We are beyond offering a feature pack or a user interface refresh.  We are bringing you a complete reengineering of SAP Ariba as an AI native platform - think fully microservices-based model that offers greater scalability and faster, lower impact innovation.  AI takes on the burden of manual processes and frees your procurement team to focus on strategic, high value business decisions. 

Join the Upcoming Session 

Date: Wednesday, July 8, 2026 

Topic: MTE - Next-gen SAP Ariba Sourcing and SAP Ariba Contracts

Time: 9:00 am – 9:30 am CDT

Language: English 

Future sessions on additional products are listed below.

Date

Topic

Time

Language

July 22, 2026

MTE: Next-gen SAP Ariba Invoicing - Live Demo

 

9:00 AM CDT

English

August 5, 2026

MTE: Next-gen SAP Ariba Buying - Live Demo

 

9:00 AM CDT

English

August 19, 2026

MTE: Next-gen SAP Ariba Supplier Management

 

9:00 AM CDT

English

 

Register for the event through the Spend Management Value Map Events and access the complete document on this topic here. 

To access Value Map Groups, you must login to SAP Community with an S-user ID or a Universal ID. 

Note: To provision a Universal ID, please follow these steps:  

(1) Visit the SAP Universal ID website 

(2) sign up for an account using your company email, and 

(3) link your existing S-user ID to your new Universal ID. 

 This session is relevant for our existing SAP Ariba customer team members: 

  • Developer 
  • Business User 
  • Administrator 
  • Architect

It will provide practical insights into the roadmap, architecture, and what steps you should start considering now. 

This is just the beginning. More events will continue to be populated under the Spend Management Value Map Events, so stay tuned and check back regularly to see what’s coming next. 

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps   

Beyond this topic, SAP Enterprise Support value maps on SAP Community provides guidance, recommended actions, and supporting resources to help you decide what to focus on next—all included in your SAP cloud subscription.   

👉Watch short demo video, then head over to  our page  explore our range of topics and request a call  with a topic expert for guidance tailored to your goals.    

About the author:

Ernie Cosper is a Topic Expert for SAP Enterprise Support value maps, specializing in Spend Management. With over a decade at SAP and prior to this another decade in consulting and implementations, she brings deep experience from a range of customer-facing and managerial roles. Ernie enjoys working with customers, partners and peers to drive value and forge success from the foundation of SAP Enterprise Support.

💡 Have questions or feedback around this content? Don’t hesitate to comment below or contact @Ernie_Cosper.

entry.title: Re: Payment Terms Limitation for Installment Payments Abo...
entry.content: Hi Elena, I wanted to come back and thank you for your help. Your suggestion was exactly the solution we needed. After following your recommendation, we successfully configured the installment payment terms from 1,020 days up to 1,800 days using Additional Months together with No. of Days. We tested the configuration with different document dates to make sure the due dates were calculated correctly, and all the results matched the expected due dates. Thanks to your guidance, we were able to solve the 999-day limitation using standard SAP configuration, without any custom development. Thank you very much for taking the time to explain the solution in such detail. Your support made a real difference and is greatly appreciated. Best regards, Priscilla Dias

entry.title: Query regarding the navigation error during creation of PO in "Process Purchase Requisitions" app.
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is there anyone encounter the error when converting purchase requisition to purchase order using "Process Purchase Requisition" application? The error prompt and saying that "Navigation to this application is not supported".

What is the possible cause of this, and can anyone give me solution?

Thank you!

entry.title: Trigger an SAP Build Process Automation Workflow from CAP Using the @cap-js/process Plugin
entry.content:
Overview

 

Previously, triggering an SAP Build Process Automation (SBPA) workflow from a CAP application required direct REST API calls. On April 26, 2026, the Node.js plugin `@cap-js/process` was released as its first beta, offering a native CAP integration. This guide walks through the complete setup step by step.

 

Prerequisites
  • Your BTP subaccount is set up for SAP Build Process Automation. Refer to the SBPA initial setup guide
  • CAP local development tools are installed. Refer to CAP initial setup .
Procedure

 

Step 1 — Develop and Deploy a Process in SBPA
Create and deploy your process in the SAP Build Process Automation designer.
 

 
After deployment, locate the workflow instance ID in the deployment details.
 

 

In this example the process definition ID is:
ap10.iac-apj-workshop-i30jhz3m.wfcap1.wfcap1
 
Note this value — it is required in Steps 5 and 7.

 

Step 2 — Generate a Sample CAP Project
cds init capwf --nodejs --add sample
cd capwf
 

 

 

Step 3 — Add the `@cap-js/process` Plugin
npm add @cap-js/process
 

 

 

Step 4 — Bind the CAP Project to the SBPA Service Instance

 

Log in to Cloud Foundry and bind the `ProcessService` to your SBPA service instance.
cf login -a https://api.cf.<cf-data-center>.hana.ondemand.com --sso
cds bind ProcessService -2 <service-instance>

 

> Replace `<cf-data-center>` with your Cloud Foundry region (e.g. `eu10`, `us10`) and `<service-instance>` with the name of your SBPA service instance.

 

 

Step 5 — Import the Process as a CDS Service

 

Use the `process_definition_id` from [Step 1].
cds import --from process --name <process_definition_id>
 

 

 

Step 6 — Open the Project in VS Code

 

 

 

Step 7 — Annotate the CDS Service to Trigger the Workflow

 

Edit `srv/admin-service.cds` to add `@bpm.process` annotations to the `Books` entity.

 

**Before:**
using {sap.capire.bookshop as my} from '../db/schema';
service AdminService {
entity Authors as projection on my.Authors;
entity Books as projection on my.Books;
entity Genres as projection on my.Genres;
}
 

 

After:
 
 

 

```cds
using {sap.capire.bookshop as my} from '../db/schema';

 

service AdminService {

 

entity Authors as projection on my.Authors;

 

@BPM.process.start : {
id : 'ap10.iac-apj-workshop-i30jhz3m.wfcap1.wfcap1',
on : 'submit',
inputs: [
{ path: $self.idStr, as: 'id' },
{ path: $self.authorName, as: 'author' },
$self.title,
$self.descr,
{ path: $self.genreName, as: 'genre' },
$self.stock,
$self.price,
{ path: $self.currencyCode, as: 'currency' }
]
}
@BPM.process.cancel #one : {on: 'DELETE'}
@BPM.process.businessKey #one: (title)
@BPM.process.businessKey : (ID)
entity Books as projection on my.Books {
*,
cast(ID as String) as idStr : String,
author.name as authorName : String,
genre.name as genreName : String,
currency.code as currencyCode: String
}
actions {
action submit();
};

 

entity Genres as projection on my.Genres;

 

}
```

 

Key annotations explained:

 

| Annotation | Purpose |
| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `@bpm.process.start` | Triggers the SBPA workflow when the `submit` action is called, mapping entity fields to process inputs |
| `@bpm.process.cancel` | Cancels the running workflow instance when the entity is deleted |
| `@bpm.process.businessKey` | Sets the business key used to correlate the workflow instance with the CAP entity |

 

 

Step 8 — Run the Application Locally

 

Start the application in hybrid mode so it connects to the bound SBPA service instance.
cds watch --profile hybrid
 

 

 

Step 9 — Test the `submit` Action

 

Create `test/http/AdminService.http` and send the following request to trigger the SBPA workflow for an existing active book.

 

@server=http://localhost:4004
@username=alice
@password=

### Books Submit action on an existing active Book (ID=201)
# @NAME Books_Submit_Existing
POST {{server}}/odata/v4/admin/Books(ID=201,IsActiveEntity=true)/AdminService.submit
Content-Type: application/json
Authorization: Basic {{username}}:{{password}}


{}

 

 

 

The terminal confirms the workflow was triggered — notice `[process] – Starting process ap10.iac-apj-workshop-i30jhz3m.wfcap1.wfcap1` and the book data passed as inputs.

 

 

Step 10 — Verify the Workflow Task in SBPA My Inbox

 

Open the SAP Build Process Automation **My Inbox**. The approval task appears with the book data (ID, author, title, description, genre, stock, price, currency) populated from the CAP entity.

 

 
The End!
 
Thank you for your time!
